task:
Work only in the strange_back repository on branch fix/vlm-media-source-contract, freshly based on origin/develop. Implement user-requested items 1 and 2 as one validation-coupled change; do not implement clip-range item 3. Preserve primary alert persistence, WebSocket, and FCM behavior, and ensure VLM enqueue failures remain side-channel failures. Do not mutate .gjc workflow state except through team APIs. The runtime-owned guard prevented writing the optional pre-context snapshot, so this task text is the grounded context: current selector wrongly prefers clipPath; enqueue currently only deduplicates and directly constructs the selector; SafetyEventDto/AlertEvent currently lack clip_object_key. Follow existing Spring/AWS/test conventions.

### Lane A — Delivery
Implement the VLM media-source contract and focused unit/regression tests. Required behavior: clip_object_key first; otherwise extract only an S3 HTTP clip_url object key; then snapshot S3 key; never select clip_path; reject Windows absolute, Unix absolute, and file:// inputs; enqueue requires eventId and source key; CLIP requires clips/ prefix; dedupe by source type/key + prompt version + model; introduce injectable MediaObjectVerifier with a test stub and production HEAD Object implementation boundary. Review all enqueue call sites so failures cannot break primary alert/WebSocket/FCM flow. Run focused tests and report changed files and commands. Coordinate conflicts through team mailbox; do not touch files outside strange_back.

### Lane B — Verification
Own independent verification and red-team review. First inspect the contract, schemas, selector/enqueue call sites, and existing tests without editing delivery-owned files. Monitor Lane A completion/state, then test the final shared worktree behavior: precedence, S3 HTTP extraction, local-path rejection (Windows/Unix/file), missing eventId/source, non-clips CLIP rejection, dedupe tuple, media verifier false/error behavior, and primary alert side-channel isolation. Add tests only in a clearly separate test file if needed and announce overlap before editing; otherwise report blockers and exact Gradle commands/evidence. Run git diff --check at terminal verification.
